claims.claim: Under heat-penetration coupled stimulations at 100°C, the average temperature rise rate of NCM811 cells increases from 1.992°C/s at 0% SOC to 93.033°C/s at 100% SOC.
claims.quote: at critical 100℃, as the SOC increases from 0% to 100%, the average temperature rise rate of the cell sharply increases from 1.992°C/s to 93.033°C/s

claims.claim: Under heat-penetration coupled stimulations at 100°C, the maximum cell temperature increases from 125.9°C at 0% SOC to 652.9°C at 100% SOC.
claims.quote: the maximum temperature of cell increases from 125.9°C to 652.9°C, and the mass loss increases from 3.332 g to 31.180 g

claims.claim: The critical SOC for flame generation in NCM811 cells subjected to nail penetration at 100°C is 25%; cells with 0%, 10%, 15%, and 20% SOC did not produce a flame under these conditions.
claims.quote: Because the 0%, 10%, 15%, and 20% SOC cell TR did not produce a flame, only the flame temperature and pattern of the 25%, 50%, 75% and 100% SOC cell are analyzed in this paper. Therefore, the critical SOC leading to flame generation under heat-penetration coupled stimulations is 25% for NCM811 cell.

claims.claim: Nail penetration of a 25% SOC NCM811 cell at 100°C produces a jet flame and elevates the maximum temperature by 76.1°C (18% higher) compared to penetration at room temperature, where no jet flame is produced.
claims.quote: penetration at room temperature, the 25% SOC NCM811 cell will not produce a jet fire, whereas penetration at 100°C, the 25% SOC NCM811 cell still produces jet fire, penetration at 100°C elevates the fire risk in contrast to room temperature. And the maximum temperature of cell was elevated by 76.1°C, which was 18% higher than that of penetration at room temperature.

claims.claim: Under heat-penetration coupled stimulations at 100°C, the TR flame temperature increases from 437.8°C at 25% SOC to 918.5°C at 100% SOC, and the maximum flame area increases from 2419.14 cm² to 9235.42 cm².
claims.quote: the flame temperature increases from 437.8°C to 918.5°C, the flame area rise ratio reaching 281.77%... the maximum area of the cell jet flame gradually increases as the SOC increases. From 25% SOC to 100% SOC, the maximum flame area was raised from 2419.14 cm2 to 9235.42 cm2

claims.claim: SEI decomposition generally occurs at about 100°C, at which point the SEI starts to decompose and regenerate, constantly releasing heat and increasing the battery's internal temperature.
claims.quote: decomposition, which generally occurs at about 100 ◦C. At this moment, the SEI starts to decompose and regenerate, constantly releasing heat and increasing the battery's internal temperature.

claims.claim: The separator, normally manufactured with polypropylene or polyethylene, melts when the battery temperature achieves 120–130°C, causing a short circuit due to the loss of protection between the anode and cathode.
claims.quote: the separator, normally manufactured with polypropylene or polyethylene, is melted when the battery temperature achieves 120–130 ◦C. A short circuit occurs due to the inexistence of protection between the anode and cathode, producing more heat according to Joule's law.

claims.claim: NMC batteries showed a maximum accumulated heat release of approximately 28 kJ and a maximum temperature rise rate of approximately 3100°C/min, while pristine LFP batteries showed a maximum accumulated heat release of approximately 5.5 kJ and a maximum temperature rise rate of approximately 1.1°C/min.
claims.quote: the maximum accumulated heat released and temperature rise rate on average were 28 kJ and 3100 ◦C/min, respectively. These values are significantly higher than those obtained for LFP batteries

claims.claim: Pristine LFP batteries did not exhibit sufficient self-sustaining exothermic reactions to induce thermal runaway during external heating tests, with maximum temperature remaining below 315°C.
claims.quote: LFP batteries did not exhibit a sufficient self-sustaining chemical reaction to induce thermal runaway during the heating tests. For this reason, the onset temperature and crucial temperature have no values. The maximum temperature was lower than 315 ◦C

claims.claim: For aged batteries, SEI growth consumes lithium and electrolyte in the anode, reducing the quantity of both components and thereby decreasing heat released during the anode-electrolyte reaction; compared to pristine conditions, heat released was lower by 12.3% for NMC and 68.9% for LFP when aged at 50°C.
claims.quote: The main outcome of this process is the reduction of the cycled lithium and the electrolyte present in the anode. The SEI growth increases the thickness using both elements, reducing the battery's capacity. Considering this process, the only influence that can occur in the heat release calculator is in Reaction (5) and Reaction (7). Both elements are directly in contact during Reaction (5), and less quantity results in less heat release. Compared with the Pristine case, Fig. 9, the heat released is lower, 12.3% for the NMC and 68.9% for the LFP.

claims.claim: The thermal safety of the LFP battery relative to NMC is attributed to the endothermic behavior of cathode decomposition and less oxygen released during this process; LFP releases approximately half the oxygen released by NMC cathodes, resulting in about 11.5 kJ less heat from electrolyte oxidation.
claims.quote: the oxygen released during this reaction is lower than the NMC battery. It resulted in a lower heat released in Reaction (4), a difference of about 11.5 kJ higher for the NMC chemistry.

claims.claim: Batteries aged at 50°C ambient temperature showed a lower maximum temperature and lower temperature rise rate during thermal runaway compared to pristine NMC batteries, but the thermal runaway process was triggered at a lower onset temperature and lower crucial temperature.
claims.quote: The average maximum temperature and temperature rise rate achieved were lower than the pristine NMC batteries. In addition, the thermal runaway process was triggered before, as illustrated by the lower onset temperature and crucial temperature.

claims.claim: NEPCM with 6% SWCNT increases the melting temperature distribution by up to 15.27% compared to pure PCM setups.
claims.quote: NEPCM with 6 % SWCNT increases the melting temperature distribution by up to 15.27 % compared to pure PCM setups

claims.claim: NEPCM with 6% SWCNT enhances the liquid fraction by up to 66.54% compared to pure PCM under similar conditions.
claims.quote: enhances the liquid fraction by up to 66.54 % under similar conditions

claims.claim: SWCNT nanoparticles demonstrate superior enhancement in thermal conductivity compared to alumina, leading to more effective heat absorption and dissipation.
claims.quote: The inclusion of SWCNT demonstrates a superior enhancement in thermal conductivity compared to alumina, leading to more effective heat absorption and dissipation.

claims.claim: PCM1, positioned adjacent to the battery, exhibits an immediate increase in temperature and melting rate, significantly outperforming PCM2 in thermal regulation.
claims.quote: PCM1, positioned adjacent to the battery, exhibits an immediate increase in temperature and melting rate, significantly outperforming PCM2 in thermal regulation.

claims.claim: The average melting fraction of PCM1 (RT82) is 55.35% higher than that of PCM2 (RT27), attributed to its proximity to the heat source.
claims.quote: the average melting fraction of PCM 1 (RT82) is observed to be 55.35 % higher than that of PCM 2 (RT27), underscoring the enhanced thermal performance facilitated by the proximity to the heat source
